text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Osei-Bonsu, Margaret
MRN: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: Interventional Radiology / Medical Oncology
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Interventional Radiology
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
  A:  Core needle biopsy, liver, segment VI, CT-guided percutaneous

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY:
The patient is a 67-year-old female with a history of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the pancreatic head/uncinate process (originally diagnosed at age 66, clinical stage T3 N0 M0), treated with neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles), followed by concurrent capecitabine-based long-course chemoradiation (50.4 Gy in 28 fractions), and pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with pathologic stage pT3 pN1 M0 (Stage IIB, R0 resection, 2/18 lymph nodes positive, moderate treatment response CAP grade 2). She subsequently received adjuvant gemcitabine plus capecitabine (6 cycles completed approximately 12 months prior). Routine surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis demonstrated a new 1.2 cm hypodense hepatic lesion in segment VI, associated with rising CA 19-9 (67 U/mL, above upper limit of normal) and CEA (4.8 ng/mL). MRI liver with hepatobiliary phase (Eovist) demonstrated arterial enhancement with portal venous washout and hypointensity on hepatobiliary phase imaging, features highly suspicious for metastatic adenocarcinoma. CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy of the segment VI lesion was performed to establish tissue diagnosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE TO BE INSERTED]
DATE OF RECEIPT: [DATE TO BE INSERTED]
DATE OF SIGN-OUT: [DATE TO BE INSERTED]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TYPE OF SPECIMEN:
CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy (18-gauge core needle; 3 cores obtained), liver, segment VI

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name, medical record number, and "liver biopsy, segment VI." The specimen consists of three tan-white, cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments ranging from 0.9 to 1.4 cm in length and approximately 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores are somewhat firm with focal areas of firm, white tissue interspersed with softer, pale tan parenchyma. There is no grossly visible necrosis. All three cores are submitted entirely for histologic evaluation in one cassette (A1).

Gross photographs: Taken.
Decalcification: Not required.
Intraoperative consultation: None requested.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Section A1 reveals three core needle biopsy fragments of hepatic parenchyma with partial replacement by metastatic carcinoma.

The background liver parenchyma shows mild macrovesicular steatosis and mild portal chronic inflammation without significant fibrosis (Ishak fibrosis score 0-1). The hepatocytes at the periphery of the tumor demonstrate pressure atrophy and mild cholestatic change.

Within the biopsy cores, there are infiltrative, irregular glandular and tubular structures embedded in a moderately cellular, desmoplastic stroma. The neoplastic glands range from small, rounded, tightly packed angulated tubules to larger, complex, irregular glandular formations with intraluminal cellular debris. The stroma is markedly fibrotic and hypocellular in some areas, with scattered spindle-shaped fibroblasts and occasional inflammatory cells (predominantly lymphocytes and plasma cells).

The tumor cells demonstrate moderate-to-severe nuclear pleomorphism with enlarged, vesicular nuclei, irregular and angulated nuclear contours, and one to several prominent macronucleoli. The nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio is increased. The cytoplasm is pale to amphophilic, and intraluminal mucin is readily identified within many of the glandular lumina, highlighted on mucicarmine stain. No squamous differentiation is identified. Mitotic figures are present (estimated 4 per 10 high-power fields), including atypical forms. Apoptotic bodies are occasionally noted.

The architectural pattern, cytologic features, and stromal desmoplasia are morphologically identical to the moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma identified in the prior pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen (pathology report on file). No evidence of hepatocellular carcinoma morphology (e.g., trabecular pattern, bile production, intracytoplasmic inclusions) is seen.

Lymphovascular invasion: Not definitively identified in the biopsy material.
Perineural invasion: Not assessable in core biopsy specimen.
Necrósis: Focal comedo-type necrosis present within larger glandular lumina.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on block A1):

  Cytokeratin 7 (CK7):          POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells)
  Cytokeratin 19 (CK19):        POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells)
  Cytokeratin 20 (CK20):        NEGATIVE (no staining in tumor cells; positive internal control present)
  CDX2:                         NEGATIVE (no nuclear staining in tumor cells)
  TTF-1 (thyroid transcription factor-1): NEGATIVE (no nuclear staining in tumor cells)
  PAX8:                         NEGATIVE (no nuclear staining in tumor cells)
  MUC1 (EMA):                   POSITIVE (moderate to strong membranous and cytoplasmic staining)
  MUC5AC:                       FOCALLY POSITIVE (weak cytoplasmic staining in a subset of tumor glands)
  SMAD4/DPC4:                   RETAINED (positive nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells; intact expression, consistent with prior biopsy and resection specimen)
  Hepatocyte (HepPar-1):        NEGATIVE
  Glypican-3:                   NEGATIVE
  Arginase-1:                   NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin:                NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A:               NEGATIVE
  p53:                          ABERRANT OVEREXPRESSION (strong, diffuse nuclear positivity in >80% of tumor nuclei; consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation, in keeping with prior molecular profiling)
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):                Proliferative index approximately 35% (hot spot areas)

The immunoprofile (CK7+/CK19+/CK20-/CDX2-/TTF-1-/PAX8-/MUC1+/HepPar-1-/Glypican-3-/synaptophysin-/chromogranin-) is consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of pancreatobiliary origin and excludes colorectal primary, pulmonary primary, renal cell carcinoma, and primary hepatocellular carcinoma.

ADDITIONAL STAINS:
  Mucicarmine:                  POSITIVE (intraluminal mucin within neoplastic glands)
  PAS with diastase:            Focal intracytoplasmic mucin present in tumor cells

MOLECULAR / IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION STUDIES:
  PENDING -- Repeat next-generation sequencing (NGS) on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue from block A1 has been ordered at the request of the treating oncologist to assess for acquired resistance mutations or newly actionable alterations at time of metastatic recurrence. Results to be reported as an addendum.

  Note: Prior molecular profiling (Tempus xT solid tumor panel, performed on original EUS-FNB specimen at time of diagnosis) demonstrated: KRAS p.G12V (VAF 42%), TP53 p.R248W (VAF 38%), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, SMAD4 wild-type, ARID1A p.Q1368* (VAF 11%), RNF43 p.G659Vfs*41 (VAF 28%), KDM6A deep deletion. TMB 3 mut/Mb (low). MSS. No BRCA1/2, ATM, or PALB2 mutations detected. No actionable fusions.

  MSI/MMR by IHC: PENDING (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 panel ordered; results to follow in addendum)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY:

A:  CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, LIVER, SEGMENT VI, CT-GUIDED (3 CORES):
      -- METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH PANCREATIC DUCTAL PRIMARY
         (see microscopic description and immunohistochemistry above)
      -- Morphology and immunophenotype identical to the previously resected moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (Whipple specimen; prior accession number on file)
      -- SMAD4/DPC4 expression: RETAINED (consistent with prior studies)
      -- Background liver: Mild macrovesicular steatosis; mild portal chronic inflammation; no significant fibrosis
      -- Molecular studies: PENDING (see above)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The histologic features and immunohistochemical profile of the hepatic lesion are entirely consistent with metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. The tumor shows identical morphology to the primary resection specimen, including moderately differentiated infiltrative glandular architecture with desmoplastic stroma, intraluminal mucin production, and aberrant p53 immunostaining consistent with TP53 mutation. Retention of SMAD4/DPC4 expression is concordant with the prior biopsy and resection specimens, arguing against clonal evolution with SMAD4 loss at the time of metastatic recurrence. The hepatocellular markers (HepPar-1, Glypican-3, Arginase-1) are negative, excluding a primary hepatocellular process. The neuroendocrine markers are negative, excluding a metastatic pancreatic neuroendocrine neoplasm.

Correlation with clinical, radiologic, and prior pathologic findings is recommended. Results of pending molecular studies will be communicated as a pathology addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name Redacted]

ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DISTRIBUTION: Medical Oncology, Interventional Radiology, Primary Care
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*** ADDENDUM PENDING: NGS (repeat solid tumor panel on metastatic specimen) and MMR IHC (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) results forthcoming. Results will be reported in a separate addendum report. ***
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
